name: Ecole du Vin at La Maison du Vin de Saint-Emilion
summary: Bookable Saint-Emilion tasting initiation led by a professional, with English/French sessions and a fixed central address.
whyItMatters: A structured tasting is easier to schedule than trying to improvise château appointments around travel days.
pros: Official tourism page says initiations are offered in French and English.
Official tourism page lists Saturday public sessions from 11:00 to 12:30 with many May-Oct 2026 dates.
cons: Fixed Saturday timing may not fit the itinerary.

logistics.opening_hours: Public sessions listed Saturday 11:00-12:30 on many May-Oct 2026 dates.
logistics.reservation_needed: Yes; tourism page links to a booking page.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until lodging/activity plan is fixed.
logistics.transit: Central Saint-Emilion village; verify train/taxi/walk timing.
logistics.parking: Unknown; verify village parking if driving.

timing.best_visit_window: Saturday morning if it lines up with the Saint-Emilion day.
timing.race_day_risk: Low unless travel from Bordeaux is disrupted by race operations.

sources.title: Saint-Emilion Tourisme - Ecole du Vin a la Maison du Vin
sources.note: Supports tasting concept, French/English availability, Saturday 11:00-12:30 public sessions, May-Oct 2026 dates, price, address, and booking link.

open_questions: Check exact available Saturday date once Saint-Emilion itinerary date is locked.
Confirm the desired language for the tasting.
